SCADA Standards Volume 3 of 3 
has four significant digits and is presumed to be accurate to the tenths place. This is 
highly dependent on the instrument or calculation. Digitals shall not be indicated past 
their determined significance. 
Numbers that might be referenced using scientific notation will allow enough space for 
the sign and exponential portion. In example: “1.246E-8” has four significant digits and 
an exponent portion indicating its magnitude.
